agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on $390,000 electric bikeshare contract extension
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposed contract amendment with Charleston Mobility that would add up to $390,000 to keep the citywide electric bikeshare program running until the end of the current fiscal year. This is a temporary move to buy time while the city decides the program's long-term future during the Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget process. The money would come from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget. The decision has not been made yet — this item was continued from an earlier meeting and is up for approval at the next council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this keeps the bikeshare running, but the program's long-term future is still undecided.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.3
Contract Amendment No. 3 with Charleston Mobility for Electric Bikeshare Operations
[image]  
APPROVE a third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility in an amount not to exceed $390,000 for a total not-to-exceed contract amount of $3,540,341, to support the continued operation of the citywide electric bikeshare program till the end of the fiscal year to allow time to determine the future of the program during the Fiscal Year 2026-27 budget process; and APPROPRIATE $390,000 from the Environmental and Community Investment Agreement (ECIA) Transportation budget to cover the cost of the contract amendment – Public Works Department/Community Services Department (Gabino Arredondo 510-620-6606/LaShonda White 510-620-6828). This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, meeting.
•

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on updated sidewalk vendor rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to consider a proposed bylaw that would update Richmond's sidewalk vendor regulations (Chapter 7.42 of the Municipal Code). The changes would revise administrative procedures, waiver provisions, and enforcement mechanisms for sidewalk vendors. This item was continued from the April 28, 2026 meeting and is now up for first reading — meaning council will introduce and debate the proposed ordinance before a final vote. If approved, the new rules could affect how and where vendors operate on public sidewalks.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, these rule changes could affect your sidewalk vending licence or operations.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.2
Introduce Ordinance Amending Sidewalk Vendor Regulations (RMC Chapter 7.42)
[image]  
INTRODUCE an ordinance (first reading) amending Chapter 7.42 of the Richmond Municipal Code (Sidewalk Vendor Ordinance) to update administrative procedures, waiver provisions, and enforcement mechanisms - Public Works Department (Andy Cho 620-6547/Robert Armijo 620-5477). This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, meeting.
•

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on opposing CO2 pipeline project
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a resolution opposing the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub. This project would involve building a large network of CO2 pipelines through Richmond to dump carbon waste underground. Councilmember Claudia Jimenez has put forward the resolution, which argues the pipelines pose a risk to the health and safety of Richmond residents. The vote was postponed from an earlier meeting and is now up for approval at the next council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Contact Councilmember Claudia Jimenez at 510-620-6565 to voice your support or concerns before the vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
Q.1
Opposition to Proposed CO2 Waste Dumping Projects & CO2 Pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties
[image]  
RECEIVE a presentation on proposed Montezuma CO2 Hub, a carbon waste dumping project, that would require a massive build out of pipelines through Richmond, risking the health and safety of Richmond residents and ADOPT a resolution that opposes the proposed Montezuma Carbon Sequestration Hub's CO2 pipeline waste dumping projects and CO2 pipelines in Richmond, Contra Costa, and Solano Counties - Councilmember Claudia Jimenez (510-620-6565). This item was continued from the April 28, 2026, meeting.
•

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider appeals of Marina Point residential project approval
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council will hold a public hearing to consider two appeals of the Planning Commission's conditional approval of the Marina Point Residential Project. Council is set to vote on whether to modify the conditions of approval or uphold the original decision. This is a proposed decision — nothing has been finalized yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing to voice your support or concerns about the Marina Point project conditions.

key_dates.label: Marina Point project public hearing — Richmond Council

agenda_briefs.source_body:
P.1
Appeal of Planning Commission Conditional Approval of Marina Point Residential Project
[image]  
•  
HOLD a public hearing to consider two appeals of the Planning Commission’s conditional approval of the Marina Point Residential Project (PLN23-117) and ADOPT a resolution Modifying the Conditions in response to the appeals and otherwise affirming the Planning Commission’s conditional approval - 
Community Development Department (Lina Velasco/ Avery Stark 510-620-6714).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond FY 2026-27 operating budget and capital plan
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider the draft Fiscal Year 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and the FY 2026-31 Five-Year Capital Improvement Plan. This is a proposed spending plan that covers day-to-day city operations for the next year and major infrastructure projects over the next five years. The draft budget has been received for review, and council will hear updates from city departments before making a final decision. No vote has been taken yet — this is the start of the discussion and approval process.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date and council vote — this budget could affect your property taxes and business costs.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
O.1
Draft Fiscal Year (FY) 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and FY 2026-31 Five-Year Capital Improvement Plan
[image]  
•  
ACKNOWLEDGE receipt of the draft Fiscal Year 2026-27 Annual Operating Budget and Fiscal Year 2026-31 Five-Year Capital Improvement Plan; REVIEW the budget adoption schedule; and RECEIVE an update on departmental operations, with discussions upon completion of all departmental presentations – City Manager’s Office/Finance Department (Shasa Curl/Emily Combs/Mubeen Qader 510-412-2077).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Sole-source contract for Ferry Point Pump Station up to $45,000
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a sole-source contract with Schaaf & Wheeler Consulting Civil Engineers for $29,690.43 to complete engineering services for the Ferry Point Pump Station Project. A sole-source contract means the city would hire this company without bidding it out to other firms. The total amount set aside for final project costs is $45,000, taken from the Wastewater Enterprise Fund. This is a proposed decision and has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from you — this is an internal city contract decision. Watch for the council vote to see if it passes.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.6.a
Contract with Schaaf & Wheeler Consulting Civil Engineers for the Ferry Point Pump Station Project
[image]  
•  
APPROVE a sole-source contract with Schaaf & Wheeler Consulting Civil Engineers for $29,690.43 for a term ending on January 1, 2028, for completed construction- related engineering services and APPROPRIATE $45,000 from the Wastewater Enterprise Fund for the Ferry Point Pump Station Project final expenditures – Public Works Department (Darcie DeLashmutt 510-672-3944/Tawfic Halaby 510-620-5482/Daniel Chavarria 510-620-5478).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $300K community prosecutor deal
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a five-year agreement with the Contra Costa County District Attorney's Office to fund a dedicated deputy district attorney — called a 'community-based prosecutor' — who would be assigned specifically to Richmond. The cost would not exceed $300,000, and the proposed term runs from July 1, 2026 to June 30, 2029. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if approved, this could mean more focused prosecution of local crime affecting your business.

key_dates.label: Proposed community prosecutor agreement start date
Proposed community prosecutor agreement end date

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.g
Agreement with Contra Costa County District Attorney's Office to Continue the Community-based Prosecution Program
[image]  
•  
APPROVE a five-year agreement with the Contra Costa County District Attorney's Office for the dedicated services of a deputy district attorney who would be specifically assigned as Richmond's Community-based Prosecutor, in an amount not to exceed $300,000, for the term commencing July 1, 2026, and ending June 30, 2029 - Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Sole-source contract for mental health services up to $525,000
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a sole-source contract with Public Safety Family Counseling Group for mental health counseling and consultant services. The proposed contract is for a three-year term starting July 1, 2026, with an option for two one-year extensions, at a total cost not to exceed $525,000 (up to $175,000 per year). This is a proposed decision — it has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.f
Contract with Public Safety Family Counseling Group for Mental Health Counseling and Consultant Services
[image]  
•  
APPROVE a sole-source contract with Public Safety Family Counseling Group, Inc. for mental health counseling and consultant services in an amount not to exceed $525,000, for a three-year term from July 1, 2026, through June 30, 2029, with two optional one-year extensions, for an annual amount not to exceed $175,000 – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802/Captain Matthew Stonebraker 510-621-1204).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $90K police background check contracts
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on approving contracts with T Jung Investigations and Steve Blanc Investigations to conduct background checks for police department job applicants. Each contract would be worth up to $90,000 over three years, starting April 15, 2026, with the option to extend for two additional one-year terms. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.

key_dates.label: Proposed police background check contracts start date — Richmond council vote pending

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.e
Contract with T Jung Investigations and Steve Blanc Investigations for Pre-Employment Background Investigators
[image]  
•  
APPROVE contracts with T Jung Investigations and, separately, Steve Blanc Investigations, to provide background investigations for Police Department applicants on an as-needed basis, in an amount not to exceed $90,000 per vendor over a three-year contract term of April 15, 2026, to April 15, 2029, with two one- year extension options – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $7.2M animal control contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract with Contra Costa County Animal Services Department for ongoing animal control services. The contract would be worth up to $7,200,000 and would cover the 2026–2027 fiscal year, with an option to extend for three more years (2027–2030). This is not yet approved — council will consider and vote on the proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time — watch for the council decision if you operate an animal-related business.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.d
Agreement with Contra Costa County Animal Services Department for Ongoing Animal Control Services
[image]  
•  
APPROVE and AUTHORIZE a contract with Contra Costa County Animal Services Department in an amount not to exceed $7,200,000 for the continuation of Animal Control Services for Fiscal Year 2026–2027, with an additional three-year term for Fiscal Years 2027–2028, 2028–2029, and 2029–2030, for a total contract term of July 1, 2022, through June 30, 2030 – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive March 2026 Richmond crime report
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to receive the monthly crime report from the Richmond Police Department for March 2026. This is a routine report that provides an overview of crime statistics and trends in the city. The report has not yet been presented or discussed — council will consider it at an upcoming meeting. Business owners should watch for any new crime patterns that could affect their operations or security.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.c
March 2026 Crime Report
[image]  
•  
RECEIVE the monthly Crime Report from the Richmond Police Department for March 2026 – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive February 2026 Richmond crime report
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to receive the monthly crime report from the Richmond Police Department for February 2026. This is a routine update — the report will be presented at a council meeting for information only. No new rules or decisions are being voted on at this time. The report will give council members and the public a snapshot of recent crime trends and police activity in the city.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.b
Monthly Crime Report for February 2026
[image]  
•  
RECEIVE the monthly Crime Report from the Richmond Police Department for February 2026 – Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond police vehicle rental contract up to $540,000
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed three-year contract with Enterprise Holdings Incorporated for the Richmond Police Department to rent unmarked police vehicles. The contract would cost up to $540,000 from May 6, 2026 to May 5, 2029, with the option for two one-year extensions worth an additional $360,000. This is a proposed decision that has not yet been approved.

key_dates.label: Proposed contract start date — Richmond police vehicle rental
Proposed contract end date — Richmond police vehicle rental

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.5.a
Contract with Enterprise Holdings Incorporated for Rental Car Services
[image]  
•  
AUTHORIZE and APPROVE a three-year contract with Enterprise Holdings Incorporated for the Richmond Police Department to rent unmarked police vehicles for an amount not to exceed $540,000 for the period of May 6, 2026, through May 5, 2029, with two one-year, mutually agreed upon annual extensions for an additional total of $360,000 — Police Department (Chief Timothy Simmons 510-621-1802).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to appoint Courtney Sanders to ECIA Transportation Board
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposal to appoint Courtney Sanders to a vacant seat on the ECIA Transportation Board. If approved, the appointment would run until July 25, 2029. The ECIA Transportation Board likely deals with transportation planning and issues in the region. This is a proposed decision and has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.4.b
Appointment of Courtney Sanders to the ECIA Transportation Board
[image]  
•  
APPOINT Courtney Sanders to a vacant seat on the ECIA Transportation Board with a term that expires on July 25, 2029 – Mayor’s Office (Mayor Eduardo Martinez 510-620-6503).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to approve $1.5M radio purchase from Motorola Solutions
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond Council is set to vote on a proposal to approve a standing purchase order with Motorola Solutions, Inc. for portable and mobile radios, up to a total of $1,500,000. The agreement would run until May 6, 2029, with the option to extend it twice for one year each, at an additional cost not to exceed $250,000. This purchase is for the Fire Department. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from business owners at this time — this is an internal City procurement decision.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.3.a
Purchase of Portable and Mobile Radios from Motorola Solutions, Inc.
[image]  
•  
APPROVE a standing purchase order with Motorola Solutions, Inc. to purchase portable and mobile radios in an amount not to exceed $1,500,000 for a term ending May 6, 2029, with the possibility of two one-year extension options in a not to exceed $250,000 – Fire Department (Chief Aaron Osorio 510-307-8021/Chief Rico Rincon 510-307-8041/Den Mark Marcelo 510-307-8038).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: $69,000 for Sister Cities delegations to China, Cuba, Palestine
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving $69,000 from the General Fund to support the Sister Cities Commission's delegation activities with partner cities in Zhoushan (China), Regla (Cuba), and Sebastia (Palestine). If approved, the city manager would be authorized to sign agreements with a third-party administrator to manage the funds. City staff would also develop a scholarship program, in partnership with the Zhoushan Sister City Commission, to help cover travel costs for participants. This is a proposed decision — it has not been voted on yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.2.b
Appropriate Funding for Sister Cities Commission Partner City Delegations
[image]  
•  
APPROPRIATE $69,000 in General Fund to the Sister Cities Commission to support delegation activities with Zhoushan, China; Regla, Cuba; and Sebastia, Palestine; AUTHORIZE the city manager, with approval as to form by the City Attorney’s Office, to enter into agreement(s) with qualified third-party administrator(s) and take all necessary actions to administer appropriated funds in support of eligible costs associated with the Richmond–Zhoushan Sister City program; and AUTHORIZE city staff to develop and implement a scholarship program, in collaboration with the Zhoushan Sister City Commission, to support travel costs – Finance Department (Emily Combs/Nickie Mastay/Shasa Curl 510-620-6740).

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive March 2026 city financial reports
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council will consider receiving several financial reports for March 2026 at an upcoming meeting. These include the Investment and Cash Balance Report, Monthly Overtime Reports, the Documentary Transfer Tax Report (a tax paid when property ownership changes hands), and reports on Other Post-Employment Benefits (OPEB) and Pensions (benefits for retired city workers). This is a routine information item — council is being asked to formally receive and note these reports, not to vote on a new rule or spending.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.2.a
Investment and Cash Balance Report, Monthly Overtime Reports, Documentary Transfer Tax Report, and Other Post-Employment Benefits (OPEB) and Pension Report for the month of March 2026.
[image]  
•  
RECEIVE the City’s Investment and Cash Balance Report, Monthly Overtime Reports, Documentary Transfer Tax Report, and Other Post-Employment Benefits (OPEB) and Pension Report for the month of March 2026 – Finance Department (Emily Combs 510-620-6740).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond to add caste as protected category in municipal code
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed ordinance that would add caste as a protected category in the Richmond Municipal Code. If approved, this change would make it illegal to discriminate against someone based on their caste under the city's human rights rules. The proposed amendment would update sections 3.58.010, 3.58.020, and 3.58.060 of the code, giving the City's Human Rights and Human Relations Commission the power to handle caste-based discrimination complaints. This is a second reading of the ordinance, meaning it has been introduced once before and is now up for final approval.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if passed, this will affect all Richmond employers and landlords.
agenda_briefs.industries: Labour & Employment, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.d
Establishing a Commitment to Caste Equity and the Prevention of Caste-Based Discrimination
[image]  
ADOPT an ordinance (second reading) amending sections 3.58.010, 3.58.020, and 3.58.060 of the Richmond Municipal Code to add caste as a protected category within the jurisdiction of the City’s Human Rights and Human Relations Commission – City Attorney’s Office (Shannon Moore 510-620-6505/Dallae Chin 510-620-5517/Brandi Robinson 510-620-5518).
•

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $150,000 legal contract extension
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a fifth amendment to the city's legal services agreement with the law firm Burke, Williams & Sorensen, LLP. If approved, this amendment would add $150,000 to the contract, bringing the total to $400,000, and extend the agreement until December 31, 2027. The firm would continue to represent the City of Richmond in municipal law matters, including personnel issues and lawsuits. This is a proposed decision and has not yet been approved.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.c
Legal Service Agreement Amendment No. 5 with Burke, Williams & Sorensen, LLP (Burke) for Municipal Litigation Matters
[image]  
•  
APPROVE a fifth amendment to the Legal Services Agreement with Burke, Williams & Sorensen, LLP, in the amount of $150,000 for a total contract amount not to exceed $400,000, and extend the term to December 31, 2027, to continue representing the City in municipal law matters, including personnel matters and litigation – City Attorney’s Office (Kimberly Chin 510-620-6709/Shannon Moore 510-620-6509).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on $500,000 legal services deal
agenda_briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed legal services agreement with the law firm Bennett, Gelini and Gelini, APC (BGG). If approved, the agreement would allow the City to hire BGG for up to $500,000 to handle litigation (court cases) and liability claims (lawsuits where someone says the City is responsible for harm). The proposed contract would run from May 5, 2026, to June 30, 2028. This is not yet approved — Council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.b
Legal Services Agreement with Bennett, Gelini and Gelini, APC (BGG) for Representation and Liability Claim
[image]  
•  
APPROVE a new legal services agreement with Bennett, Gelini and Gelini, APC, for litigation representation and liability claims in an amount not to exceed $500,000 with a term of May 5, 2026, through June 30, 2028 – City Attorney’s Office (Kimberly Chin 510-620-6709/ Shannon Moore 510-620-6505).

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond council to vote on $100K legal services deal
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a legal services agreement with the firm Colantuono, Highsmith & Whatley, PC. The agreement would cover general litigation (court cases) and municipal advice (legal guidance for the city) for up to $100,000. The proposed term runs from May 5, 2026, through December 31, 2027. This is a proposed decision and has not yet been approved.

key_dates.label: Proposed legal services agreement term starts
Proposed legal services agreement term ends

agenda_briefs.source_body:
N.1.a
Legal Services Agreement with Colantuono, Highsmith & Whatley, PC for General Litigation and Municipal Advice
[image]  
•  
APPROVE a legal services agreement with Colantuono, Highsmith & Whatley, PC, for general litigation and municipal advice in an amount not to exceed $100,000, for a term of May 5, 2026, through December 31, 2027 – City Attorney’s Office (Kimberly Chin 510-620-6709/ Shannon Moore 510-620-6509).
